quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: Hummingbirds are the only birds that can fly backwards! They can also hover in place by flapping their wings up to 80 times per second, creating that distinctive humming sound. The Pacific Northwest is home to Anna's hummingbird, which stays year-round even in winter.

short_read.title: The Secret Lives of Hummingbirds
short_read.teaser: These tiny jewels of the bird world have hearts that beat over 1,200 times per minute and memories that help them remember every flower they've visited. Discover the amazing adaptations that make hummingbirds such extraordinary creatures.

medium_read.title: Hungary's Lake Balaton: Central Europe's Beloved Sea
medium_read.teaser: Lake Balaton has been a cherished summer destination for Hungarians for generations, with its warm shallow waters and charming lakeside towns. This journey explores the lake's natural beauty, historic villages, and the traditions that make it the heart of Hungarian summer life.

long_read.title: Olympic National Park: A Wilderness Like No Other
long_read.teaser: From temperate rainforests to glacier-capped peaks to wild Pacific beaches, Olympic National Park contains an extraordinary diversity of ecosystems found nowhere else. This deep dive explores the unique geology, rare wildlife, and ancient forests that make the Olympics a natural treasure right in your backyard.

port_angeles.title: Harbor Seal Pupping Season on the Strait
port_angeles.description: July is prime time to spot harbor seal pups along the Strait of Juan de Fuca! Mother seals often rest with their pups on rocky shores and beaches around Port Angeles. Remember to observe from a distance—these adorable youngsters are learning important survival skills from their mothers.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart can beat up to ___ times per minute during flight.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Exactly right, Barbara! That's an incredibly fast heartbeat—about 20 times faster than a human's!
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! The answer is 1,200 beats per minute—their tiny hearts work amazingly hard!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: The answer is 1,200 beats per minute! It's hard to imagine a heart beating that fast, but hummingbirds need all that energy for their amazing flying abilities.
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: To fuel such a fast metabolism, hummingbirds must eat about half their body weight in sugar each day, visiting hundreds of flowers from dawn to dusk.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'thank you'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Köszönöm! That's perfect, Barbara! It's such a lovely word to say.
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The answer is 'köszönöm'! Szia means 'hi,' viszlát means 'goodbye,' and igen means 'yes'—all useful words too!
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungarian is a unique language in Europe, belonging to the Finno-Ugric family rather than the Indo-European languages that surround it. It's most closely related to Finnish and Estonian.

puzzle_3.question: What type of tree dominates the Hoh Rainforest in Olympic National Park?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Wonderful, Barbara! Sitka spruce trees create the magical canopy of the Hoh Rainforest, some growing over 300 feet tall and draped in emerald moss.
puzzle_3.wrong_response: It's actually Sitka spruce! These magnificent giants thrive in the moist climate, creating the enchanted, moss-covered forest that makes the Hoh so special.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: The Hoh Rainforest receives 12-14 feet of rain each year, making it one of the wettest places in the continental United States. This creates the perfect conditions for the lush, fairy-tale landscape of ancient trees and hanging moss.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
